title: cystopteris fragilis var. canariensis
intro: Cystopteris fragilis var. canariensis, also known as the Canary Island Cystopteris, is a rare and delicate fern species native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Canarian Islands, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant are said to include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and providing antimicrobial protection. Cystopteris fragilis var. canariensis can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it may also be used in other herbal preparations, such as infusions or salves.
